content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string AAA and want to convert it to another binary string BBB. Both have lengths NNN.

You can do either of the following operations at most ‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ times:



**Type 111**: Choose (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N, and flip AiA_iAi‚Äč for each L‚Č§i‚Č§RL \le i \le RL‚Č§i‚Č§R. To flip XXX means to replace it with 1‚ąíX1 - X1‚ąíX.




**Type 222**: Choose (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N, and reverse the subarray A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R]. Formally, simultaneously replace AiA_iAi‚Äč with AL+R‚ąíiA_{L + R - i}AL+R‚ąíi‚Äč for all L‚Č§i‚Č§RL \le i \le RL‚Č§i‚Č§R.



Find a sequence of operations of length at most ‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ to transform AAA to BBB. It can be proven that at least one valid sequence exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains the value of NNN.
The second line contains the binary string AAA.
The third line contains the binary string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



First, KKK (0‚Č§K‚Č§‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ0 \le K \le \lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil0‚Č§K‚Č§‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ) the number of operations you will perform.
Now output KKK lines, each with 333 integers: type, LLL and RRR (1‚Č§type‚Č§2,1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le type \le 2, 1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§type‚Č§2,1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N), representing the type and range of the operation performed.

If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 200020002000 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Initially, A=1110A = 1110A=1110. We first flip the range [2,4][2, 4][2,4] to get 100110011001. Then we reverse the range [3,4][3, 4][3,4] to get 101010101010. Now, A=BA = BA=B and we used 222 operations (which is exactly how many we were allowed).
